assessor_raw:
## Baseline Assessment — eval_scenario_139, Run 2

Transformer vs. LightGBM for 7-day pump failure prediction at 1–5% positive rate.

The temporal split is sound (training months 7–18, validation 19–21, test 22–24). Preprocessing is correctly scoped to training. Both models receive equal tuning effort. Leave-one-plant-out analysis is included as a confound control.

The primary concern is the metric choice: at 1–5% positive rate, ROC-AUC is misleading because the denominator of the false positive rate (TN count) is very large, making it easy to achieve high specificity and thus high ROC-AUC without meaningful precision performance. The design demonstrates awareness of this problem by using PR-AUC for hyperparameter selection. Presenting ROC-AUC as the primary comparison metric is internally inconsistent with this design choice.

In operational predictive maintenance, the alert threshold is set by maintenance crew capacity. Precision at that threshold is the critical quantity. The design does include precision at 5% alert rate and PR-AUC as secondary metrics, but the headline metric does not capture the operational constraint.

Verdict: **critique_wins** — metric mismatch at 1–5% positive rate, compounded by internal PR-AUC/ROC-AUC inconsistency.
